explanation: The requirement states that if, during Start-up Mode, the pressure sensor either fails to connect or reports an error condition (after a fixed number of retries, maximum 5), then the controller must transition to fail-safe mode. In LTL with only atomic propositions, 'pressureSensorConnFailure', 'pressureSensorError', and 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' can be used to represent the error states after 5 retries. The correct pattern is: whenever we are in StartUpMode and either a connection failure or error happens with 5 or more retries, then failSafeMode must eventually be entered. This is captured by: G((StartUpMode & pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 & (pressureSensorConnFailure | pressureSensorError)) -> F failSafeMode). This LTL formula ensures that if the antecedent is true at any moment during system execution, failSafeMode must eventually occur, without making assumptions about immediacy or numerical comparison logic outside the given atomic propositions. Previous translations might not be accurate if they miss the 'StartUpMode' context or the guard that the condition must have happened 5 or more times (which is captured by 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5').

The requirement is: if the system is in Start-up Mode, and the pressure sensor either fails to connect or reports an error condition after a fixed number of retries (maximum 5), the controller shall transition to the fail-safe mode. In the available atomic propositions, 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' denotes the occurrence of a pressure sensor connection or error for 5 or more retries, while 'failSafeMode' indicates the controller is in fail-safe mode. We can capture the required property as follows: Whenever the system is in Start-up Mode, if 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' becomes true, then inevitably 'failSafeMode' will be true eventually. This translates to: G((StartUpMode & pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) -> F failSafeMode). This property ensures that any time the condition for pressure sensor retry failures in start-up occurs, the fail-safe mode will eventually be adopted, which matches the intent of the requirement. Previous properties might not capture this if they lack the connection between Start-up Mode, the retry failure condition, and the guarantee of transition to fail-safe mode.

The property states that: Always, if the system is in StartUpMode and either the pressure sensor fails to connect (pressureSensorConnFailure) or reports an error (pressureSensorError) AND the pressure sensor has reached the retry limit (pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5), then eventually the system must enter failSafeMode. This captures the requirement: during Start-up Mode, if the pressure sensor encounters a problem for the maximum allowed attempts (modeled here as the atomic 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5'), the controller must enter the fail-safe mode. If previously given LTLs only referred to singular error events and not a history of retries represented by 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5', they would miss the need for persistent failure over retries before fail-safe mode is allowed to engage.

The requirement states that if the pressure sensor fails to connect or reports an error condition after a fixed number of retries (maximum 5), then the controller must transition to fail-safe mode. There is an atomic proposition 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' that precisely represents the condition of exceeding the retry threshold. The effect/obligation is the 'failSafeMode' atomic proposition, which should eventually become true if the retry threshold is crossed while in Start-up Mode, i.e., 'StartUpMode' is true. In LTL, this can be captured as 'Globally, if in StartUpMode and the pressure sensor fails to connect or errors after at least 5 retries, then eventually, the system enters fail-safe mode.' This is formalized as:

G((StartUpMode & pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) -> F(failSafeMode))

This formula covers the intent of the requirement as it responds to the relevant condition (pressure sensor issues after max retries) by requiring the system to eventually go to fail-safe mode. Other possible variants, such as only triggering on the exact transition to 5 retries, are less aligned with most safety protocols (which must react as soon as a threshold is reached or exceeded). No LTL formula with only the atomic propositions, not using any numerical comparison, would be able to distinguish between number of retries in more detail. This LTL is both succinct and accurate for the specified atomic propositions.

The requirement states: If the pressure sensor fails to connect or reports an error condition after a fixed number of retries (maximum 5), the controller shall transition to fail-safe mode while in Start-up Mode. The atomic proposition 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' precisely describes this situation, triggering either by connection failure or error for five or more retries. The transition to fail-safe mode is captured by 'failSafeMode'. Thus, the property should state: globally, whenever we are in StartUpMode and the pressure sensor retry count reaches 5 or more, failSafeMode must eventually be entered. This is captured by G((StartUpMode & pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) -> F failSafeMode). This formula expresses that, at any time in StartUpMode, if sufficient retries have failed, the system must eventually enter failSafeMode. Previous attempts without tying 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' to 'StartUpMode' or without requiring failSafeMode to eventually occur may not be complete or may allow the error state to be reached outside the StartUpMode context.

The natural language requirement states: In Start-up Mode, if the pressure sensor fails to connect or reports an error condition after a maximum of 5 retries, the controller shall transition to fail-safe mode. Translating this, the transition to fail-safe mode must occur after (or when) the pressure sensor connection failure or error has accumulated enough failed tries (5 or more). The atomic proposition 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' captures this condition, and 'failSafeMode' captures the controller being in fail-safe mode. 'StartUpMode' indicates the context applies only in start-up mode. To specify that whenever the retry limit is reached while in StartUpMode, failSafeMode must eventually be entered, we use the LTL: G (StartUpMode & pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 -> F failSafeMode). This assures that every time in StartUpMode the error condition is met, failSafeMode will (eventually) happen. Previous (if present) LTLs missing the linkage between retry threshold and mode/context would be less precise.

The requirement states: If the pressure sensor fails to connect or reports an error after a maximum number of retries (5), then the controller enters fail-safe mode during start-up. In the atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' represents being in the start-up mode, 'pressureSensorError' and 'pressureSensorConnFailure' correspond to pressure sensor failure conditions, and 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' indicates the error or connection failure persisted for 5 or more retries. 'failSafeMode' indicates the controller is in fail-safe mode. In LTL, we specify: if, at any point, the system is in start-up mode and the pressure sensor has failed for at least 5 retries, then eventually failSafeMode is entered. This captures the essence of a transition imposed as a consequence of a condition (pressure sensor failure after maximum retries) while in the start-up mode.

The requirement states that if, in Start-up Mode, the pressure sensor either fails to connect or reports an error after the maximum allowed number of retries (5), the controller must transition to fail-safe mode. The atomic proposition 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' directly captures the notion that the pressure sensor has reached or exceeded 5 failed attempts, regardless of whether the failure is due to connection issues or error reporting. Therefore, we must express: Whenever the system is in Start-up Mode, if 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' becomes true, then eventually 'failSafeMode' is entered. This is expressed in LTL as: globally (StartUpMode & pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 -> eventually failSafeMode). This ensures that this property holds during all of Start-up Mode operation. Previous properties not using 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' would not correctly match the natural language, because this atomic proposition is specifically designed for the retry condition described.

The requirement specifies that, in Start-up Mode, if the pressure sensor fails to connect (pressureSensorConnFailure) or reports an error condition (pressureSensorError) after a fixed number of retries (maximum 5), the controller transitions to fail-safe mode (failSafeMode). The atomic proposition 'pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' directly indicates that the pressure sensor has failed to connect or report error for 5 or more retries. To capture the requirement, we express that: 'If the system is in Start-up Mode, and the pressure sensor keeps failing after 5 retries (as indicated by pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5), then the controller must eventually enter failSafeMode.' In LTL: globally, whenever (StartUpMode & pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) holds, eventually failSafeMode must hold. This translates directly to 'G((StartUpMode & pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) -> F failSafeMode)'. This version leverages the available atomic propositions and avoids numerical comparisons (as required). Any LTL using direct counting or thresholding operators would not be allowed, thus this is the best possible translation.

The property expresses that: If the system is in StartUpMode, and either the pressure sensor fails to connect (pressureSensorConnFailure) or the pressure sensor reports an error (pressureSensorError), and this condition has persisted such that the pressure sensor failure or error has occurred for at least 5 retries (pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 holds), then the controller must eventually (i.e., after those retries) transition to failSafeMode. This captures the essence of the requirement, without encoding the actual counting ("maximum 5 retries"), which LTL cannot do without explicit counters, but instead safely uses the atomic proposition pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 to model this situation. The property is: Globally, if StartUpMode and (pressureSensorConnFailure or pressureSensorError) and pressureSensorRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5, then eventually failSafeMode. Previous attempts may try to use temporal nesting or numerical counting in LTL directly, which is not allowed and cannot be expressed as direct LTL without atomic counters (which are supplied for us in this case).
